title: androsace axillaris
intro: Androsace axillaris, also known as the Alpine Rock Jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Androsace axillaris typically involve drying the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures. Additionally, the plant's roots and stems are also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
